reasoning: While the fidelity score is high, a major structural defect exists where a 16-member roll call vote table was truncated to a single entry, representing a significant loss of official record data. Additionally, the flattening of numerous structured lists into dense paragraphs and the disagreement between programmatic signals and LLM fidelity regarding table existence necessitate human intervention to ensure the integrity of these board minutes.

concerns.description: Fabricated tables: baseline has 0 tables but output has 3

concerns.analysis: The table 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' at element index 18 corresponds to the vote roster on page 10 of the source. The source table contains 16 data rows (one for each board member). The output JSON only contains one data row (David Bone), omitting the remaining 15 members. This is a material omission of approximately 94% of the table's data rows.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table on page 10 contains 16 data rows for board members Tommy Cole, Greg Coltrain, Brian Drew, Bo Ferguson, Greg Foster, Chuck Greene, J.D. Hartman, Melanie Jones, Lee Kerlin, Jeff Ledford, John Moore, Jude O'Sullivan, Jeff Shipp, Earl Struble, Jim Weaver, and Donna Wright.
concerns.output_quote: The data rows in the table at element index 18 only include: { "cell_type": "data", "content": [ { "text": "David Bone..." } ], ... }
concerns.location: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE, Page 10, element index 18

concerns.analysis: The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table on page 10 is severely truncated. The source table lists 16 board members and staff, providing voting records for each. The output JSON only contains a single data row (David Bone), omitting approximately 15 rows of data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table on page 10 contains rows for all board members (David Bone, Tommy Cole, Greg Coltrain, Brian Drew, Bo Ferguson, Greg Foster, Chuck Greene, J.D. Hartman, Melanie Jones, Lee Kerlin, Jeff Ledford, John Moore, Jude O'Sullivan, Jeff Shipp, Earl Struble, Jim Weaver, Donna Wright).
concerns.output_quote: The table object at element index 18 only contains two rows: one header row and one data row for 'David Bone'.

concerns.analysis: The document contains numerous structured lists (numbered items 1-14, lettered subsections a-j, and bulleted progress reports). In the output, these lists are entirely flattened into single, massive paragraph blocks. The semantic meaning and structural hierarchy of these lists are lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source pages 3-9 use distinct numbering (1., 2...) and lettering (a), b)...) for meeting items, as well as bullet points for grant updates.
concerns.output_quote: Content is merged into paragraph blocks such as element index 12, which combines items 1 through 5b into a single text block.

concerns.analysis: Hyperlinks present in the source document (e.g., 'Live Status Map' on page 3 and 'SIP Admin' on page 8) are rendered as plain text in the output. While the text is accurate, the link functionality is missing. Per instructions, missing links are a minor structural defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 3: 'Live Status Map' is a blue hyperlink. Page 8: 'SIP Admin' is a blue hyperlink.
concerns.output_quote: The words 'Live Status Map' and 'SIP Admin' appear in paragraph text without a corresponding URL or clickable markup.

action_items: Restore the missing 15 rows of the roll call vote table on page 10.
Convert flattened paragraph blocks into properly formatted HTML lists to match the source structure.
Restore functional hyperlinks for 'Live Status Map' and 'SIP Admin'.
